message:
LIKE prefix range optimization with upper bound computation

Add upper bound computation for LIKE prefix index scans. New
like_prefix_upper_bound() function computes the exclusive upper
bound (e.g., "abc" -> "abd"), enabling the planner to model
LIKE 'abc%' as the range column >= "abc" AND column < "abd".

WhereTermKind::LikePrefix now carries an upper_bound: Option<String>
alongside the prefix. IndexUsability::LikePrefix renamed fields from
{ prefix } to { low, high: Option<String> } to represent the full
range. All LIKE-related planner tests updated to verify upper bound
computation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
